text:
sought to be treated equally, and that itself would be a negation 
of the principle of substantive equality. Therefore, it can easily be 
inferred that the rule relating to practice or in the alternative, to 
secure 70% in the first attempt in the examinations, is a case of 
indirect discrimination as the provisions are facially neutral but 
discriminatory in operation. In view of the same, Rule 7 of the 
Madhya Pradesh Judicial Service Rules, 1994, to the extent it 
prescribes the additional requirement of either a three-year practice 
period or securing an aggregate score of 70% in the first attempt, 
is liable to be struck down insofar as it applies to PwD candidates. 
Accordingly, the impugned Rule will be applicable to PwD candidates 
insofar as it prescribes the educational and other qualifications as 
eligibility criteria including the minimum aggregate score of 70% 
(with relaxation as may be determined like in the case of SC/ST
